SearchDialog.title: Search
SearchDialog.description: Search projects, essays, paintings, and pages. Type to filter the archive.

Footer.statement: Building serious tools for agent work. Writing about everything that resists becoming a tool.

ProjectsPage.metadataDescription: Agent-native systems, local-first developer tools, products, and open-source work by Abdul Hamid Achik.
ProjectsPage.metadataKeywords: Abdul Hamid Achik, projects, open source, agent tools, MCP, Cortex, Cairntrace, Glyphrun, TinyVault, MCPHub, Bob, CLI tools, Go
ProjectsPage.title: Independent systems, not isolated demos.
ProjectsPage.description: A working catalogue of agent infrastructure, local-first command-line tools, product experiments, libraries, and editors. The newest work forms one connected evidence stack—from repository creation to browser and terminal verification.
ProjectsPage.selectedDescription: Five independent tools that connect planning, context, and behavioral evidence.

Chat.title: Concierge
Chat.welcome: You are speaking with Mr. Smerdyakov, Dostoevsky's character. I suppose you have questions about this archive. What would you like to discuss?

ErrorPage.title: Something interrupted the page
ErrorPage.description: The page could not finish loading. You can try again or return home.

EssaysPage.title: Essays, notes, and unfinished arguments.
EssaysPage.description: Long-form writing on software, literature, psychoanalysis, painting, politics, exile, and the unconscious—in English, Spanish, and Russian.
EssaysPage.searchPlaceholder: Search by title, idea, or tag…

HomePage.title: Tools for agents. Essays for humans.
HomePage.description: I build local-first systems that make software agents more capable, observable, and accountable. I also write about literature, psychoanalysis, painting, politics, and the things no tool can resolve.
HomePage.authVerified: Your email is verified. The concierge is ready to continue the conversation.
HomePage.authInvalidToken: This verification link is invalid or has expired. Request a new one from the concierge.
HomePage.authVerificationFailed: We could not verify that link. Please request a new one from the concierge.
HomePage.deployedTitle: The workshop extends beyond the featured build graph.
HomePage.deployedDescription: The opening index shows one active toolchain. This is the complete public deployment map: agent infrastructure, developer tools, products, libraries, and experiments you can use now.
HomePage.deployedCount: {count, plural, =1 {# live site} other {# live sites}}
HomePage.systemTitle: A toolchain for work that has to prove itself.
HomePage.systemDescription: The projects are designed as a system: frame the work, coordinate context, verify behavior, and keep the runtime legible. Each tool stays useful alone; together they close the evidence loop.

frame.title: Frame the work
frame.description: Turn a small product contract into a deterministic, reviewable foundation with explicit ownership.

coordinate.title: Coordinate context
coordinate.description: Keep goals, evidence, memory, and MCP infrastructure coherent across long-running agent sessions.

verify.title: Verify behavior
verify.description: Run browser and terminal behavior contracts against real interfaces and preserve the resulting evidence.

operate.title: Operate locally
operate.description: Observe the machine, protect secrets, retain artifacts, and run agents without surrendering the whole workflow to a cloud service.

HomePage.writingTitle: The other half of the work.
HomePage.writingDescription: Long-form notes where software gives way to art, language, politics, exile, and the unconscious. Written in English, Spanish, and Russian.
HomePage.emptyWriting: The next essay is still taking shape.
HomePage.aboutTitle: Software needs evidence. Writing needs pressure.
HomePage.aboutDescription: I am a developer and writer based in Guadalajara. The software work is concerned with durable agent systems; the archive follows literature, psychoanalysis, painting, and political life wherever they touch the same nerve.
